action.reasoning: Observation: Iteration 14 crashed because model.py's MLP class no longer matched the legacy best_512.pt checkpoint (state dict mismatch with BatchNorm keys). Iteration 15 inspected the configs but didn't fix the code. Analysis: The legacy checkpoint best_512.pt contains keys for a deeper MLP with BatchNorm layers (net.1, net.5, net.9 are BatchNorm, net.0/4/8/12 are Linear). We need a LegacyMLP class to load it. Meanwhile, the three newer MLP checkpoints (mlp_seed42/123/456) are compatible with the current MLP class and have high val_ics (~0.036). Decision: Add LegacyMLP to model.py to recover best_512.pt. Update predict.py to gracefully try MLP then LegacyMLP fallback when loading checkpoints, skipping any that still fail. This makes the ensemble robust. Run local predict to verify the fix works, then submit evaluation.
